---
title: "hw 5 demo"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-12"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```




```{r}
rm(list = ls())
```


Homework 5

Read in the data

```{r}
setwd("C:\Users\ottenheimew\Desktop\examplefolder")
```


```{r}
#read.csv()

covidcountydf <- read.csv(file.choose(), header = TRUE)

```


```{r}
### Look for the demographic county data stored on your pc
uscntydemo <- read.csv(file.choose())
```


```{r}
wbhdf <- read.csv(file.choose())

wbccc <- read.csv(file.choose())
```


import the required packages (GGPLOT IS REALLY HELPFUL HERE)

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
```

combine data sets Use merge
```{r}
mergedcovid <- merge(covidcountydf,uscntydemo, by.x = "County", by.y = "County.name")


```

create new variables

```{r}
mergedcovid$caseperpop <- mergedcovid$Cases/mergedcovid$TotalPop
mergedcovid$deathperpop <- mergedcovid$Deaths/mergedcovid$TotalPop
mergedcovid$casepersnrs <- mergedcovid$Cases/mergedcovid$Pop.Over65
```

decreasing order
```{r}

#desc function is useful here

decreasingdeathdf <- arrange(mergedcovid,desc(mergedcovid$deathperpop))
```

compare original and new df heads
```{r}
head(mergedcovid)
```

decreasing
```{r}
head(decreasingdeathdf)
```

Something doesn't add up how could there be more deaths than people?

The issue is some states have the same county name.

```{r}
mergedcovid <- merge(covidcountydf, uscntydemo, by.x = c("County","State"), by.y = c("County.name","ST.Name"))
```


```{r}
mergedcovid$caseperpop <- mergedcovid$Cases/mergedcovid$TotalPop
mergedcovid$deathperpop <- mergedcovid$Deaths/mergedcovid$TotalPop
mergedcovid$casepersnrs <- mergedcovid$Cases/mergedcovid$Pop.Over65
```

decreasing order
```{r}

#desc function is useful here
decreasingdeathdf <- arrange(mergedcovid,desc(mergedcovid$deathperpop))
```

```{r}
head(mergedcovid)
```

decreasing
```{r}
head(decreasingdeathdf)
```
```{r}
print(mean(mergedcovid$deathperpop))
```
```{r}
WBHDFcodeonly <- data.frame(wbhdf[,1])
names(WBHDFcodeonly) <- c("code")
```

```{r}
tidywbhdf <- WBHDFcodeonly %>%  
  separate(col = code, into = c("group","subgroup","Measurement","units"), sep = "\\.", fill = "right", remove = FALSE) 
```


Is this tidy?

Every variable is a column 
Every observation is a row
Each value is in one cell 

Import Petal Data

```{r}
## ready plot data
irisdf <- read.csv(file.choose(), header = FALSE)



```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
```


```{r}
names(irisdf) <- c("sepalL","sepalW","petalL","petalw","class")
```


Box Plot

```{r}
### base function

baseplot <- boxplot(irisdf$sepalW )
baseplot
print(summary(irisdf$sepalW))
```
```{r}
plotlength <- ggplot(data = irisdf, aes(y = irisdf$sepalL)) + geom_boxplot()+ coord_cartesian() + ggtitle(label = "Sepal Length Box Plot")
plotlength
```

```{r}
### change axis dimensions
plotlength <- ggplot(data = irisdf, aes(y = irisdf$sepalL)) + geom_boxplot()+ coord_cartesian(ylim = c(2,5))
plotlength
```



```{r}
plot1 <- ggplot(data = irisdf, aes(x = irisdf$class,y = irisdf$sepalW)) + geom_boxplot() + ylim(0,5) + ggtitle(label = "My boxplot") + xlab(label = "Iris Class") + ylab(label = "Length in CM") + geom_jitter()

plot1
```


Histograms

```{r}
basehist <- hist(irisdf$sepalW)

basehist
```
```{r}
gghist <- ggplot(data = irisdf, aes(x = irisdf$sepalL)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = .09 , color = "Green") + ggtitle(label = "My Histogram")

gghist
```

```{r}
gghist <- ggplot(data = irisdf, aes(x = irisdf$sepalL, shape = factor)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = .11 , color = "Green") + geom_freqpoly() + ggtitle(label = "My Histogram")

gghist
```

```{r}
gghist <- ggplot(data = irisdf, aes(x = irisdf$sepalL)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = .2 , color = "Green") + geom_density()  + ggtitle(label = "My Histogram")

gghist
```


```{r}
irisdf$factor <- as.factor(irisdf$class)
```

```{r}
classplot <- ggplot(data = irisdf, mapping = aes(fill = factor, x = sepalW)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = .15, color = "black")+facet_wrap(~irisdf$factor) + geom_freqpoly()

classplot
```
